case_title: AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK VS. CAROLINE S LEE ET AL

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION OF NON SERVICE ON CAROLINE S LEE (TRANSACTION ID # 15039025) FILED BY PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK A UTAH STATE CHARTERED BANK
ANSWER TO COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 15062039) FILED BY DEFENDANT LEE, CAROLINE S AKA CAROLINE SOON LEE, AN INDIVIDUAL
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SET FOR SEP-09-2015 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK'S MOTION FOR ORDER ENTERING JUDGMENT IS DENIED. BECAUSE THE PARTIES' STIPULATION RECITES THAT THE DEBT ALLEGEDLY OWED BY DEFENDANT CAROLINE LEE IS DISPUTED, PLAINTIFF'S EFFORT TO OBTAIN A JUDGMENT FOR THE FULL AMOUNT OF THE ALLEGED DEBT RATHER THAN THE UNPAID AMOUNT PER THE PARTIES' SETTLEMENT RUNS AFOUL OF THE CASE LAW ON UNENFORCEABLE LIQUIDATED DAMAGES PROVISIONS IN SETTLEMENT AGREEMENTS. (VIATECH INTERNATIONAL, INC. V. SPORN (2017) 16 CAL. APP. 5TH 796). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600 =302/HK

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-20-2019 Off Calendar

Motion For Order Entering Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Vivian Leung Lerche, Tel. No. 1-415-680-5731 specially appearing for the
Plaintiff;
No other appearances on the matter.


RULING - The matter was discussed. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff American Express Centurion Bank's motion for order entering judgment is denied. Because the parties' stipulation recites that the debt allegedly owed by defendant Caroline Lee is disputed, plaintiff's effort to obtain a judgment for the full amount of the alleged debt rather than the unpaid amount per the parties' settlement runs afoul of the case law on unenforceable liquidated damages provisions in settlement agreements. (Viatech International, Inc. v. Sporn (2017) 16 Cal. App. 5th 796). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
